last updated on: 28.jun.26

re-touching everything around here + life update

「28.jun.26」

i think since around march i've been working on the rework of this site. i initially wanted to upload everything once i was done with everything but given how busy i've gotten that would mean spending months without updating some pages that i use with frequency like the journal and scrapbook. and i don't like that. i've set up archive.doqmeat.com so if there's anything you're looking for from the previous version, you can find it there. there hasn't been a log in here since february and there is so much that i want to talk about! which is the main reason as to why i will be making pages live as i finish them.

doqmeat rework

this site is more than 3 years old! this site has grown to be massive, with 300-ish folders and 4k-ish files. which means this has made it pretty hard to maintain when i want to edit layouts and such. while i can live with copy and pasting HTML templates, editing THOSE templates has been a bit of a pain in the ass. i've gotten wayy more comfy with static site generators like 11ty and templating engines such as liquidJS (I LOVE YOU LIQUIDJS). i've been slowly BUT SURELY updating the code of each of my pages, giving them a new look if i think it's needed, and FINALLY getting rid of iframes.

it's been really slow but i really like it and it is SO much easier to update layouts. you guys have no idea. there's also some other really neat features like being able to save collections of pages, iterate through them. honestly a god send for pages like the journal and gaming log.

while i love my pages to be full of things, i've also grown fond of minimalist pages. i set up my current homepage as something temporary but the more i look at it the more i love it. i might or might not make a more elaborate one in the future. we will see.

important announcements (for me)

another semester of uni complete! i am very proud to announce that after rambling about how i was lowkey failing my computer architecture class i was able to pass the class with 80%. the labs, projects, AND the take home FINAL exam really saved my ass for my class. i really liked that class overall, messing around with assembly was pretty fun. i also really liked my graphic design class, it taught me how to pirate and use adobe products. yay! i think i am 50% done with my bachelor's degree at this point.

i got back into rollerskating. i used to rollerskate a bit around 2020 - 2021 but eventually dropped it once i moved out from where i was. i've been meaning to get into a hobby that was a bit more active + did not involved looking at pixels on a screen (which is every past time i have) and so i picked up some new skates and started rollerskating again. i missed it, and my body probably missed it too.

I QUIT MY RETAIL JOB! got a new job recently at my uni that involves websites so i'm really excited for that! peers that have seen my website recommended me the job, and i managed to make a good impression :] i'm quite honestly pretty scared but i know it beats being a retail worker so i'm up for the challenge! plus the experience i get from this job will also help me out when i, eventually, make the backend and database for the siinamota fanlisting and other projects. i need to get my hands more dirty on backend stuff, though i will say that my final project for my database class which was a PHP website was pretty fun.

for important stuff that is pretty much it. there's more i want to say but that's for another day!

any replies or comments are welcome on my e-mail or :3
